task_updates.executor_notes: Updated ClipAction to derive selected state from context-driven props, distinguish primary selection with border-sky-400 and secondary selection with ring-2 ring-sky-300/60, and keep click-selection out of onClick. Verified with a jsdom render probe that video, audio, and text clips produce distinct primary, secondary, and unselected class output.

task_updates.executor_notes: Wired empty-area selection clearing through the marquee path in TimelineEditor without reintroducing click-selection logic. Verified with a jsdom marquee probe that a plain empty-area click emits clearSelection while an actual drag still emits selectClips, and confirmed via source checks that VideoEditorShell already uses selectedClipIds.size > 0, batch delete, and clearSelection, while single-clip PropertiesPanel state still derives from primaryClipId in useTimelineState.

task_updates.executor_notes: Wired useMarqueeSelect into TimelineEditor and rendered the marquee overlay inside TimelineCanvas with the planned border and fill styling. Verified from the hooked code path that marquee coordinates are computed in scroll-adjusted canvas space using scrollLeft and scrollTop and that the overlay is rendered from marqueeRect inside the scroll content wrapper, preserving correct positioning while scrolled horizontally or vertically.

Extended DragSession with draggedClipIds and clipOffsets, bootstrapped multi-drag from the current selection when the anchor clip is already selected, fell back to a single draggedClipIds entry for non-selected anchors, suppressed cross-track activation for multi-drag, and added an anchor badge showing the clip count. Verified with one jsdom probe that a non-selected drag starts with draggedClipIds=['clip-1'] and selects only that clip on pointer-up, and a second probe that a selected anchor starts with both clip IDs, computes stable delta offsets, leaves cross-track ghosting disabled, and renders the count badge on the anchor element.

{ draggedClipIds: [...session.draggedClipIds], sourceRowId: session.sourceRowId } : null; }; return React.createElement('div', { ref: timelineWrapperRef }, React.createElement('button', { className: 'clip-action', 'data-clip-id': 'clip-1', 'data-row-id': 'V1' }, 'clip-1')); } const root = createRoot(document.getElementById('app')); await act(async () => { root.render(React.createElement(App)); }); const clip = document.querySelector('.clip-action'); await act(async () => { clip.dispatchEvent(new PointerEvent('pointerdown', { bubbles: true, pointerId: 1, button: 0, clientX: 10, clientY: 10 })); globalThis.captureSession(); window.dispatchEvent(new PointerEvent('pointermove', { bubbles: true, pointerId: 1, button: 0, clientX: 30, clientY: 12 })); window.dispatchEvent(new PointerEvent('pointerup', { bubbles: true, pointerId: 1, button: 0, clientX: 30, clientY: 12 })); }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ sessionSnapshot, selectCalls })); await act(async () => { root.unmount(); });"

node --import tsx/esm -e "import React, { useRef } from 'react'; import { act } from 'react'; import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client'; import { JSDOM } from 'jsdom'; import { useClipDrag } from './src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts'; globalThis.IS_REACT_ACT_ENVIRONMENT = true; const dom = new JSDOM('
'); globalThis.window = dom.window; globalThis.document = dom.window.document; globalThis.navigator = dom.window.navigator; globalThis.HTMLElement = dom.window.HTMLElement; globalThis.Element = dom.window.Element; globalThis.Node = dom.window.Node; globalThis.PointerEvent = dom.window.MouseEvent; let sessionSnapshot = null; let badgeText = null; const coordinator = { update: () => ({ time: 0, rowIndex: 1, trackId: 'V2', trackKind: 'visual', trackName: 'V2', isNewTrack: false, isReject: false, newTrackKind: null, screenCoords: { rowTop: 48, rowLeft: 0, rowWidth: 400, rowHeight: 48, clipLeft: 0, clipWidth: 100, ghostCenter: 50 } }), end: () => undefined, get lastPosition(){ return null; } }; function App(){ const timelineWrapperRef = useRef(null); const dataRef = useRef({ rows: [{ id: 'V1', actions: [{ id: 'clip-1', start: 0, end: 1, effectId: 'effect-clip-1' }, { id: 'clip-2', start: 2, end: 3, effectId: 'effect-clip-2' }] }, { id: 'V2', actions: [] }], tracks: [{ id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'V1' }, { id: 'V2', kind: 'visual', label: 'V2' }] }); const { dragSessionRef } = useClipDrag({ timelineWrapperRef, dataRef, moveClipToRow: () => undefined, createTrackAndMoveClip: () => undefined, selectClip: () => undefined, selectedClipIdsRef: { current: new Set(['clip-1', 'clip-2']) }, coordinator, rowHeight: 48, scale: 1, scaleWidth: 100, startLeft: 0 }); globalThis.captureSession = () => { const session = dragSessionRef.current; sessionSnapshot = session ? { draggedClipIds: [...session.draggedClipIds], clipOffsets: session.clipOffsets.map(({ clipId, rowId, deltaTime }) => ({ clipId, rowId, deltaTime })) } : null; const button = document.querySelector('.clip-action'); badgeText = button?.textContent ?? null; }; return React.createElement('div', { ref: timelineWrapperRef }, React.createElement('button', { className: 'clip-action', 'data-clip-id': 'clip-1', 'data-row-id': 'V1' }, 'clip')); } const root = createRoot(document.getElementById('app')); await act(async () => { root.render(React.createElement(App)); }); const clip = document.querySelector('.clip-action'); await act(async () => { clip.dispatchEvent(new PointerEvent('pointerdown', { bubbles: true, pointerId: 1, button: 0, clientX: 10, clientY: 10 })); window.dispatchEvent(new PointerEvent('pointermove', { bubbles: true, pointerId: 1, button: 0, clientX: 40, clientY: 28 })); globalThis.captureSession(); window.dispatchEvent(new PointerEvent('pointerup', { bubbles: true, pointerId: 1, button: 0, clientX: 40, clientY: 28 })); }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ sessionSnapshot, hasCrossTrackGhost: Boolean(document.querySelector('.timeline-drag-ghost')), badgeText })); await act(async () => { root.unmount(); });"

task_updates.executor_notes: Updated ClipAction context menus so right-clicking within the current multi-selection shows batch mute and delete actions for the selected set, while right-clicking a non-selected clip first replaces the selection and then shows the single-clip menu. Verified with a stateful jsdom harness that right-clicking a non-selected clip yields a menu containing Delete Clip and not the batch Delete N clips action.
commands_run:
node --import tsx/esm -e "import React, { useState } from 'react'; import { act } from 'react'; import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client'; import { JSDOM } from 'jsdom'; import { ClipAction } from './src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx'; globalThis.IS_REACT_ACT_ENVIRONMENT = true; const dom = new JSDOM('
'); globalThis.window = dom.window; globalThis.document = dom.window.document; globalThis.navigator = dom.window.navigator; globalThis.HTMLElement = dom.window.HTMLElement; globalThis.MouseEvent = dom.window.MouseEvent; function Harness(){ const [selectedIds, setSelectedIds] = useState(['clip-a', 'clip-b']); const isSelected = selectedIds.includes('clip-c'); return React.createElement(ClipAction, { action: { id: 'clip-c', start: 0, end: 2, effectId: 'effect-clip-c' }, clipMeta: { track: 'V1' }, pxPerSecond: 100, startLeft: 0, colorClassName: 'bg-blue-500/70', trackKind: 'visual', clipLabel: 'Clip C', isSelected, isPrimary: selectedIds[0] === 'clip-c', selectedClipIds, onSelect: (clipId) => setSelectedIds([clipId]), onDeleteClip: () => undefined, onDeleteClips: () => undefined, onToggleMuteClip: () => undefined, onToggleMuteClips: () => undefined }); } const root = createRoot(document.getElementById('app')); await act(async () => { root.render(React.createElement(Harness)); }); const clip = document.querySelector('.clip-action'); await act(async () => { clip.dispatchEvent(new dom.window.MouseEvent('contextmenu', { bubbles: true, cancelable: true })); }); const menuText = document.body.textContent; console.log(JSON.stringify({ menuText, hasBatchDelete: menuText.includes('Delete 2 clips'), hasSingleDelete: menuText.includes('Delete Clip') })); await act(async () => { root.unmount(); });"

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Verified with a jsdom ClipAction render probe that the primary selected clip emitted border-sky-400 styling while the secondary selected clip emitted ring-2 ring-sky-300/60, and the unselected text clip kept the baseline styling, so the visual states remain distinguishable across video, audio, and text examples.

Verified with a jsdom useMarqueeSelect probe that an empty-area click emitted clearSelection and a separate empty-area drag emitted selectClips, producing [{"kind":"clear"},{"kind":"select","ids":[]}], so background clearing does not interfere with marquee initiation.

Verified from the wired code path that marqueeRect is computed in scroll-adjusted canvas coordinates using scrollLeft and scrollTop in useMarqueeSelect, and the overlay is rendered from that marqueeRect inside TimelineCanvas's scroll content wrapper, keeping the box aligned while the timeline is scrolled horizontally or vertically.

Verified with a jsdom useClipDrag probe that dragging a non-selected clip initialized draggedClipIds to ["clip-1"] and pointer-up emitted selectClip("clip-1"), confirming the drag session does not inherit unrelated prior selections.

Verified with a stateful jsdom ClipAction harness that right-clicking a non-selected clip while other clips were selected replaced the selection first and then showed the single-clip menu; the resulting menu text contained Delete Clip and did not contain the batch Delete N clips action.
